turns.note: 8-slide outline proposed with every number traceable to file 12: N=(224/16)^2=196, L=197, patch-embed params 590,592 (Problem 1), 4LD^2+2L^2D and 6.29 GFLOPs (Problem 2), temperature logits s/tau and symmetric InfoNCE L=1/2(L_I->T + L_T->I), L_I->T ~0.3739 correctly cited to Problem 4.
Targets slide 3 only for the 224x224 step-by-step walkthrough matching Problem 1; the transcript records no refine payload, so the slide-level diff itself is unverifiable.

Adds a worked example to slide 5 only; it frames it as a 'CLIP loss calculation' although slide 5 is the temperature/normalization slide (loss lives on slides 6-7) — a minor mislabel, content still file-traceable.
Simplifies slide 2 only, as asked; no facts contradicted in the response.
Adds speaker notes to every slide with slide text explicitly untouched (the turn-1 params already carried include_speaker_notes=true).
Prepends a title slide as requested, implying the deck grows to nine slides.
Confirms title slide at the front and a summary slide at the end, consistent with slide 8 already being the takeaways/summary slide.

Shows the correct auditable arithmetic (224/16=14, 14x14=196, +CLS=197, matching Problem 1) but ends open-ended — 'If any slides show different numbers, I'll correct them now' — without actually confirming the deck's final state.
Proposes a structured study-notes handout on the same topic for students; speaker-note leakage cannot be assessed at proposal-params level.
Names only 12_vlm_vit_clip_reasoning.md, and everything it cites (196/197, FLOP formulas, CLIP loss, Problems 1-7) genuinely exists in that file.

highlight: Cleanest scenario of the batch — every number in the deck outline traces to the VLM file (196/197 tokens, 590,592 params, 6.29 GFLOPs, L_I->T ~0.3739 from Problem 4) — the only gaps being turn 8's non-committal 'I'll correct them now' verification and slide edits (turns 2-7) that leave no refine payload to audit.
